title: salsola tomentosa subsp. stellulata
intro: Salsola tomentosa subsp. stellulata, also known as Russian thistle or prickly saltwort, is a species of flowering plant in the amaranth family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which can help to treat various health issues such as wounds, infections, and respiratory problems. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity. Salsola tomentosa subsp. stellulata is typically prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed orally or used topically to harness its medicinal properties.
